\boxed{The Sahara Desert}

In *Fata Morgana* (1971), Werner Herzog juxtaposes sweeping desert landscapes (primarily the Sahara) with Lotte Eisner’s narration of the *Popol Vuh*, the K’iche’ Maya creation myth, to evoke themes of myth, time, and human insignificance. The film’s visual and auditory contrast underscores the desert’s timelessness and the universal resonance of mythic storytelling. This choice aligns with Herzog’s signature blend of natural grandeur and philosophical inquiry.
